title: Treasury Stock — Purchase and Reissue
event: Reyes Co. purchases 1,000 of its own shares at $20 each, then later reissues 400 of them. Prepare both entries (cost method).

lines.label: 1,000 × $20 cost
lines.why: Treasury stock records at COST and sits as a contra-EQUITY account.
lines.trap: Not an asset — a company cannot own itself. It reduces total equity.

lines.label: paid
lines.trap: Outstanding shares fall; issued shares don't — treasury shares are issued-but-not-outstanding.

lines.label: 400 × $25
lines.trap: Compare to the treasury COST (20/share), not to par or original issue price.

lines.label: 400 × $20 cost
lines.trap: Credit at COST regardless of the reissue price — the account never carries gains.

lines.label: 400 × $5
lines.why: The excess is contributed capital, not income.
lines.trap: NEVER a gain — companies don't earn profit trading their own shares.

lines.label: 1,000 × $20 cost
lines.why: Cost method: record what was paid.
lines.trap: Contra-equity — it appears as a NEGATIVE in the equity section.

lines.label: paid
lines.trap: Same first entry either way; the reissue price is where the variants diverge.

lines.label: 400 × $16
lines.trap: The 1,600 shortfall must come from equity — the only question is which account.

lines.label: shortfall absorbed
lines.why: The deficiency draws down PIC–Treasury first (assume a sufficient balance); RE absorbs any excess beyond it.
lines.trap: Never a 'Loss on Treasury Stock' — deficiencies reduce equity accounts, not income.

lines.label: 400 × $20 cost
lines.trap: Cost in, cost out — the treasury account is a parking lot, not a scoreboard.

memorize.body: Cost method rules: in at cost, out at cost. Reissue differences flow to PIC–Treasury (and RE if PIC runs dry) — never to income.
Treasury purchases reduce OUTSTANDING shares — which raises EPS. That's often the strategic point of the buyback.

questions.prompt: Treasury shares bought at $20 are reissued at $25 (400 shares). How much is credited to Paid-In Capital, Treasury Stock?

distractors.feedback: That's the FULL reissue proceeds (400 × $25). Only the $5-per-share excess over cost goes to Paid-In Capital, Treasury Stock — the $8,000 cost portion credits Treasury Stock itself. And never book the excess as a GAIN: equity dealings stay out of income.
That's the COST portion (400 × $20) — it credits TREASURY STOCK to remove the shares at cost. The $2,000 excess over cost is what lands in Paid-In Capital, Treasury Stock.

build.scaffold: In at cost, out at cost; the price difference lands in PIC–Treasury. The gain account in the bank is a trap.
